auth_notes: Authentication is managed via an API Key passed in the HTTP headers (typically 'Authorization: Bearer [TOKEN]' or 'x-api-key').
rate_limits: Typically 60 requests per minute per instance, though specific limits may vary based on account tier.
gotchas: Documentation is primarily provided via Swagger/OpenAPI which may lack detailed usage examples.
Pagination follows a limit/offset pattern that must be manually handled in n8n loops.
Custom fields are often returned as an array of objects rather than flat keys, requiring data transformation.
client_must_provide: List of custom field IDs for mapping
research_notes: Since no native node exists, the integration must use the HTTP Request node. Users will need to configure the 'Header Auth' credential type in n8n. Webhooks must be manually registered within the 29 instances settings panel pointing to the n8n Webhook node URL.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Since no native node exists, the integration must use the HTTP Request node. Users will need to configure the 'Header Auth' credential type in n8n. Webhooks must be manually registered within the 29 instances settings panel pointing to the n8n Webhook node URL.
